name: Roast Rack of Lamb
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the chine bone and separate the rack into two halves.
2. Cut off the ends of the bones with a cleaver or saw, reserving them for soup.
3. Trim off excess fat from the top of the meat, leaving a thin layer. Place the bones in the bottom of a roasting pan.
4. Place the meat, fat side up, on top of the bones.
5. Season with salt, pepper, and thyme.
6. Roast at 450 degrees for 30 minutes, until rare to medium-rare. Remove from the pan and keep warm, leaving the bones in the pan.
7. Set the pan over a moderate flame to caramelize the juices and clarify the fat. Pour off the fat.
8. Add garlic to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
9. Add stock and stir to deglaze the pan and dissolve the drippings. Heat until reduced by half.
10. Strain through a chinois, reserving bones for stock degreasing as needed. Season to taste.
11. Spread softened butter on the tops of the racks. Combine garlic, breadcrumbs, and parsley to make a persillade.
12. Pack the tops of the racks with the persillade.
13. Brown under the salamander.
14. Cut between ribs into chops.
15. Plate 2 chops per portion with 1 ounce jus. Serve hot.
